name: Magnetic Chess
author: João Pedro Neto & Claude Chaunier (1996) / AGP port
description: Standard chess plus a magnetism rule: after a piece moves, the closest piece along each rank and file is pulled toward it (opposite colour) or pushed away from it (same colour); the two kings are neutral and block the field. There is no check, checkmate or stalemate -- you win by capturing the enemy king. No en passant; a pawn on either of its two home ranks may still take a double step (magnetism can push pawns backward), and a pawn dragged onto the last rank by the field promotes to a Queen. A Recognized Variant by João Pedro Neto and Claude Chaunier (1996). White (player 0) moves first.
